title: Advisory Severity Distribution and Risk Scoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracks Cisco PSIRT security advisories affecting managed devices, providing evidence of vulnerability awareness and remediation tracking as required by NIST 800-53 Flaw Remediation.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates continuous monitoring of vendor-published security advisories for all managed network infrastructure.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search over index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory scheduled daily; advisory counts and affected device lists exported to compliance dashboard.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Contributes to vulnerability scanning by tracking known vendor-published vulnerabilities, though it does not replace active scanning tools.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides a vendor-authoritative vulnerability feed for network infrastructure to complement active vulnerability scanning.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard panel showing advisory severity distribution and affected device counts, refreshed with each TA poll cycle.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory`. from the `securityadvisory` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/security-advisory/advisory`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per advisory-device pair. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `advisoryId` (string), `advisoryTitle` (string), `severity` (string), `cveId` (string), `deviceCount` (integer), `deviceId` (string), `fixedVersions` (array of strings), `publicationDate` (string). CIM data model: Vulnerabilities.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" | stats dc(advisoryId) as advisory_count dc(deviceId) as affected_devices by severity | eval risk_weight=case(severity="CRITICAL",10,severity="HIGH",7,severity="MEDIUM",4,severity="LOW",1,1==1,0) | eval weighted_risk=advisory_count*risk_weight | sort -weighted_risk
description: Calculates a weighted risk score based on advisory severity and affected device count, providing a prioritized view of organizational vulnerability exposure.
value: Raw advisory counts do not reflect actual risk. Weighting by severity provides a more accurate picture of organizational exposure for executive reporting.
implementation: Enable the `securityadvisory` input. Adjust `risk_weight` numbers to match your risk team’s model; optionally multiply by `affected_devices` for a population-aware score instead of `advisory_count` alone.
visualization: Bar chart (weighted_risk by severity), table combining advisory_count, affected_devices, and weighted_risk.
knownFalsePositives:
**Risk score calculation skewed by a few CRITICAL advisories affecting many devices.** The risk scoring formula that weights severity times affected devices may produce a disproportionately high score if one CRITICAL advisory affects 100+ devices. Distinguish by breaking down the risk score by advisory to identify which single advisory is driving the score. Do not suppress — the score is mathematically correct, but provide context on which advisory contributes most.

**INFORMATIONAL advisories included in the severity distribution.** INFORMATIONAL advisories (vendor recommendations, configuration best practices) may be included in the severity distribution chart without representing actual vulnerabilities. Distinguish by checking whether `severity=INFORMATIONAL` entries have `deviceCount=0`. Suppress by filtering `| where severity!="INFORMATIONAL"` for vulnerability dashboards.

**Advisory severity assessment changing between Catalyst Center versions.** Catalyst Center may apply different severity assessment methodologies across versions, causing the severity distribution to shift after an upgrade. Distinguish by checking whether the severity distribution changed coincident with a Catalyst Center upgrade. No suppression needed — re-baseline the distribution after the upgrade.

**Duplicate advisory entries for the same CVE under different advisory IDs.** Some CVEs may be referenced by multiple Cisco advisory IDs (e.g., a combined advisory and a per-component advisory). Distinguish by checking `| stats dc(advisoryId) by cveId | where count>1`. Suppress by deduplicating on `cveId` when computing vulnerability counts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **securityadvisory** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory`.
• Sufficient advisory data (at least one poll cycle) for severity distribution analysis.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls the security advisory API at the configured interval. Advisory severity distribution and risk scoring use the severity and device count fields from each advisory event.

Key fields:
• `severity`: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFORMATIONAL.
• `deviceCount`: affected devices per advisory.
• `advisoryId`: unique advisory identifier.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory"
| stats dc(advisoryId) as advisory_count sum(deviceCount) as total_affected_devices by severity
| eventstats sum(advisory_count) as grand_total_advisories sum(total_affected_devices) as grand_total_devices
| eval advisory_pct=round(advisory_count/grand_total_advisories*100,1)
| eval device_pct=round(total_affected_devices/grand_total_devices*100,1)
| eval risk_weight=case(severity="CRITICAL",10, severity="HIGH",5, severity="MEDIUM",2, severity="LOW",1, 1==1,0)
| eval weighted_risk=risk_weight * total_affected_devices
| sort risk_weight desc
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`dc(advisoryId) as advisory_count`**: Counts distinct advisories per severity level — prevents re-polled advisories from inflating the count.
• **`sum(deviceCount) as total_affected_devices`**: Total device exposure per severity level. This is the primary risk metric.
• **`advisory_pct` and `device_pct`**: Shows the proportion of advisories and device exposure at each severity level.
• **`weighted_risk`**: Combines severity weight with device count to produce a risk score per severity band. CRITICAL × device count produces the highest risk.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Security Advisories. Check the severity distribution summary against the Splunk results.
• **Risk score sanity check:** The weighted risk should be dominated by CRITICAL and HIGH severities. If MEDIUM or LOW dominate, review the advisory classification.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Severity distribution pie chart (by advisory count and by affected devices), weighted risk score single-value tile, and a trend chart showing risk score changes over 30 days.
• **Executive reporting:** Use the severity distribution for monthly security posture reports.
• **Risk acceptance:** Advisories with zero device count or INFORMATIONAL severity can be filtered from the risk score calculation.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Risk score appears inflated:** A single CRITICAL advisory affecting many devices can dominate the score. Break down by advisory to identify the top contributors.
• **INFORMATIONAL advisories skewing the distribution:** Filter with `| where severity!="INFORMATIONAL"` for vulnerability-focused dashboards.
• **Duplicate CVEs under different advisory IDs:** Deduplicate on `cveId` when computing vulnerability counts.
• **No securityadvisory events arriving:** verify the `securityadvisory` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **Missing fields or zero values in severity:** some advisory fields are only populated for certain PSIRT types; NULL values for `fixedVersions` indicate no patch is available.
• **Fewer records than expected:** the PSIRT scan may still be running for large inventories; if credential access is denied, check the service account permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We calculate a risk score for your network based on how many security warnings exist and how serious they are, giving you one number to track improvement.
